figures.caption: Figure 7: Example 2 showing the deterministic and ambiguous tokens.

Figure 2: Training and inference architectures for our method. The LLM backbone and the lm_head remain fixed. (a) During training, the encoder predicts the appropriate latent by looking at the next token. (b) During training, a policy network predicts the appropriate latent. Policy is trained using SFT for behavioral cloning.

Figure 5: Histogram of the token entropies in predicted text for the OmniMath dataset. Two modes can be observed: one for near-deterministic tokens and another for tokens with multiple possible options.
Figure 4: Evaluation curves comparing OLLM and state-of-the-art LORA modules.

Figure 6: Example 1 showing the deterministic and ambiguous tokens. Tokens with probabilities > .85 are marked as deterministic (dark) and ambiguous (light) otherwise. Note how the start of phrases, sentences, or math expressions is ambiguous, but the endings are mostly deterministic.
